metadata.title: OSSA Agent Manifest — OSCAL Component Definition

components.title: OSSA Agent Manifest (v0.4)
components.description: Declarative YAML manifest defining an AI agent's identity, capabilities, security controls, and lifecycle. Enforced by the OSSA CLI and UADP registry.

control-implementations.description: OSSA manifest security controls mapped to NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5

implemented-requirements.description: Access Enforcement: `spec.autonomy.allowed_actions` defines an explicit allowlist of permitted agent actions. `spec.autonomy.blocked_actions` provides a blocklist. Both are enforced by the scaffold runtime — not by the LLM.

Least Privilege: `spec.autonomy.mode: supervised` restricts the agent to human-approved actions only. `spec.tools[].permissions` scopes per-tool access to the minimum required operations (e.g., read only).
Remote Access: `spec.constraints.network: internal_only` restricts the agent's network access to internal resources. `none` disables all network access. Enforced at the container/sandbox level.

Event Logging: `spec.observability.tracing.enabled` enables OpenTelemetry trace emission. `spec.observability.security_events` emits structured security events (action.attempted, action.blocked, prompt_injection.detected) to OTEL-compatible collectors.

Audit Record Generation: OSSA security events provide per-action audit records including agentId, toolName, action, timestamp, and blocking reason — consumable by SIEMs without agent-specific plugins.

Device Identification and Authentication: `metadata.x-signature` with `type: did` enables DID-based cryptographic identity for agents, resolved via `did:web:` or `did:key:` using the UADP trust verification endpoint.
Authenticator Management: `metadata.x-signature.publicKey` carries the verification key. Key revocation is propagated in real time via the `uadp.agents.trust-invalidated` AsyncAPI channel.
Boundary Protection: `spec.constraints.network` controls agent network egress (none | internal_only | allowlist). Works with container network policies for enforcement.

Public Key Infrastructure: `metadata.x-signature` supports Ed25519, ECDSA, RSA-PSS, JWT, VC, and DID as trust schemes. Verification uses @noble/ed25519 and jose — both widely audited open-source libraries.
Protection of Information at Rest: `metadata.sbom_pointer` references an externally generated SBOM (CycloneDX / SPDX) describing all agent dependencies, supporting supply chain risk management.

Malicious Code Protection: `spec.safety.guardrails` enforces pattern-based content filtering on agent inputs and tool outputs, blocking known prompt injection attack patterns before they reach the model context.

Software, Firmware, and Information Integrity: `metadata.x-signature.value` is computed over the canonical manifest (RFC 8785 json-canonicalize). Consumers verify this before executing the agent, providing manifest integrity assurance.

Developer Testing and Evaluation: The OSSA CLI provides `ossa validate` for schema-time security control verification, and integrates with @stoplight/spectral-cli for OpenAPI spec linting in CI pipelines.

System Recovery and Reconstitution: `spec.constraints.rollback_policy: automatic` signals scaffold orchestrators (Temporal, n8n) to execute compensating transactions when an agent trajectory is flagged for rollback.
